title: vaccinium gaultheriifolium
intro: Vaccinium gaultheriifolium, also known as Gaultheria, is a small, evergreen shrub native to North America, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and berries, which contain compounds like arbutin and ursolic acid. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, offering benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. Vaccinium gaultheriifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and coughs.
